hero.name: Ludovic Bostral

profile.title: What I do
profile.intro: I publish streaming intelligence reports on Lens: Vertical Invasion, Africa Streaming, Africa Landscape, Vertical Drama Market. All data accessible via the MCP server. This data feeds into concrete playbooks - launch strategy, positioning, pricing, distribution - that I help teams deploy on the ground.
profile.also: Long-form essays and a weekly streaming newsletter.
profile.award: Alain le Bussy Award 2026 (joint first prize) for the short story "Lume-saudade". Learn more
profile.side: Since February 2026, I also run Le Wagon, a bar on quai de la Fosse in Nantes. I build AI tools for bar management there. If you're in hospitality in Nantes, let's talk.

meta.title: About - Ludovic Bostral
meta.description: Ludovic Bostral's background: 25 years in streaming, from M6 Replay to Streaming Radar. Awards, publications and profiles.

about.title: About
bio: I'm Ludovic Bostral, a streaming analyst and the founder of Streaming Radar, based in Nantes, France. For 25 years I've been building and analyzing streaming platforms, from M6 Replay to emerging markets.

I started as a developer, then lead developer, at Manreo (2000-2007), before joining M6 Web (2007-2014) as a developer then architect, working on M6 Replay and 6play. In 2014 I co-founded Afrostream, an Afro-focused SVOD service that went through Y Combinator (S15 batch), as CTO. In 2015 I was part of the early team at Molotov, working on live and replay video.

I then served as CTO of Majelan (podcasts), VP Engineering at Pixagility (OTT platforms, TV over IP), CTO of Kessel (the French Substack, dedicated to newsletters), and Platform Manager at TRACE+, an SVOD service available in 40+ countries.

Since 2023 I publish streaming intelligence reports on Lens through Streaming Radar: data, playbooks and an MCP server. I also founded Vertical Drama, covering vertical shows, and created FFMPuzzle in 2026.

distinctions.title: Awards
items: Alain le Bussy Award 2026 (joint first prize) for the science-fiction short story "Lume-saudade", out of 255 submissions. To be published in Galaxies #96, October 2026. Official results.
